findings.problem: Eli's speech keeps returning to the same caretaker-imperative pattern across text, voice note, and phone call. Because the ask scene depends on him feeling both comforting and managing, the repeated 'eat / unclench / put your back against a wall / tell me what you need' rhythm starts to read as one adaptive script instead of three distinct pressure beats, which flattens his voice into mechanical calibration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate Eli's responses at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:261, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:279, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:390 so each medium does separate work. Keep his curated warmth, but let one beat carry concrete logistics or guilty self-exposure, let one beat stay brief and destabilized, and reserve the full body-management comfort routine for only one passage.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the opening ask, the post-wire voice note, and the later phone call are clearly distinguishable by rhythm and function when read aloud: at least one of the passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:261, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:390 contains concrete logistical detail or a self-exposing stumble instead of another soothing imperative, and no two beats reuse the same body-management command pattern. Fail if all three still sound like variations of the same perfectly calibrated caretaker script.

findings.problem: The chapter leans on clipped noun-fragment emphasis often enough to become a prose tic. Fragments like 'Thirty-one hundred.' and the later stack 'Rain. Fridge. The tailor's radio.' hit hard once, but by the fourth and fifth recurrence the syntax is doing the same pressure job every time and starts brushing against the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the repeated fragment beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:31, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:82, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:316, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:420 so at least two of them land through full syntax, fresh rhythm, or sharper sensory continuation rather than isolated noun fragments. Keep one strategic hammer-blow if wanted, but vary the rest so emphasis does not default to the same clipped construction.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:316, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:420 read as complete sentences or a meaningfully varied syntax mix, and if the chapter no longer relies on more than two standalone noun-or-number fragment beats for emphasis. Fail if three or more of those cited passages remain isolated fragment hits performing the same rhetorical job.

summary: A tight, consequential chapter with strong financial legibility, intact continuity, sustained Lena interiority, and admirably unsanitized economic shame. It clears the craft bar, but award-level quality is still held back by one dialogue issue and one prose issue: Eli's caretaker language repeats across mediums until it feels mechanically over-calibrated, and the chapter's clipped fragment emphasis recurs often enough to become a noticeable tic.
